Hemoglobin.description: Oxygen-carrying protein in red blood cells. Low indicates anemia; high indicates polycythemia.

WBC.description: White blood cells count. High indicates infection, inflammation, or leukemia; low indicates bone marrow failure or autoimmune conditions.

Glucose.description: Fasting blood sugar level. High indicates diabetes or prediabetes; low indicates hypoglycemia.

Potassium.description: Essential electrolyte for muscle and heart function. Extremes can cause life-threatening cardiac arrhythmias.

Sodium.description: Crucial electrolyte for fluid balance and nerve conduction. Low values can cause swelling or confusion.

LDL Cholesterol.description: Low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (bad cholesterol). High levels increase cardiovascular risk.
